gloss.en: kind and helpful

words.example: She was a benevolent old lady.

gloss.en: not straight; curved

words.example: a piece of bent wire

gloss.en: a small soft fruit with seeds

words.example: Birds eat the red berries in winter.

gloss.en: next to or at the side of

words.example: He sat beside her all night.

gloss.en: used to add an extra point

words.example: I don't really want to go. Besides, it's too late now.

gloss.en: to give something as an honour

words.example: The king bestowed a title on him.

gloss.en: a book that sells very well

words.example: Her new novel is a bestseller.

gloss.en: to risk money on a result

words.example: I bet ten dollars on that horse.

gloss.en: to confuse someone greatly

words.example: The new rules bewilder many students.

gloss.en: a feeling of being very confused

words.example: She stared at me in total bewilderment.

gloss.en: on the far side of; further on

words.example: Snowdon and the mountains beyond were covered in snow.

gloss.en: unfair favour toward one side

words.example: There is a clear bias in the news report.

gloss.en: to offer a price for something

words.example: A German firm bid for the company.

gloss.en: the act of offering prices at a sale

words.example: The bidding for the painting was fierce.

gloss.en: a two-piece swimsuit for women

words.example: She wore a red bikini at the beach.

gloss.en: a paper showing money you owe

words.example: I forgot to pay the gas bill.

gloss.en: one thousand million

words.example: Worldwide sales reached 2.5 billion.

gloss.en: a short account of a person's life

words.example: Please write a short bio for the website.

gloss.en: connected with biology or living things

words.example: She studies the biological sciences.

gloss.en: using living cells in industry and medicine

words.example: Biotechnology helps us make new medicines.

gloss.en: a cage for keeping birds

words.example: The parrot sat quietly in its birdcage.

gloss.en: the place where someone was born

words.example: This town is the birthplace of a famous poet.

gloss.en: having a strong, unpleasant taste

words.example: Black coffee leaves a bitter taste in the mouth.

gloss.en: in a strong, painful or angry way

words.example: She cried bitterly when he left.

gloss.en: responsibility for something bad

words.example: Why do I always get the blame for everything?

gloss.en: a warm cover used on a bed

words.example: It's cold tonight—can I have another blanket?

gloss.en: a large, strong fire

words.example: Firefighters fought the blaze all night.

gloss.en: not able to see

words.example: Doctors think he will go blind.

gloss.en: without thinking or seeing clearly

words.example: Don't follow the rules blindly.

gloss.en: the state of not being able to see

words.example: The disease can cause blindness.

gloss.en: to shut and open your eyes quickly

words.example: The bright light made her blink.

gloss.en: the blood flowing through the body

words.example: The drug enters the bloodstream quickly.

gloss.en: covered with blood; violent

words.example: It was a long and bloody war.

gloss.en: having open flowers; flourishing

words.example: The garden is full of blooming roses.

gloss.en: a flower on a fruit tree

words.example: The cherry blossom is beautiful in spring.

gloss.en: a hard hit with the hand or a weapon

words.example: She received a severe blow on the head.

gloss.en: to make or become unclear

words.example: Tears began to blur her eyes.

gloss.en: to say something suddenly without thinking

words.example: He blurt out the secret by mistake.

gloss.en: very hot

words.example: It was boiling hot in the kitchen.

gloss.en: brave and confident

words.example: It was a bold move to open a business in France.

gloss.en: a metal bar that fastens a door

words.example: He slid the bolt across the door.

gloss.en: to attack with many bombs or questions

words.example: Reporters bombard the star with questions.

gloss.en: a plane or person that drops bombs

words.example: The bomber flew low over the city.

gloss.en: an attack using bombs

words.example: There were recent bombings in major cities.

gloss.en: a connection between people

words.example: There is a strong bond between the sisters.

gloss.en: very thin, with bones showing

words.example: The old man had long, bony fingers.

gloss.en: an arrangement made in advance

words.example: I made a booking for two nights.

gloss.en: interested in books and study

words.example: He was a quiet, bookish child.

gloss.en: a small thin book with information

words.example: The booklet explains how to use the machine.

gloss.en: something to mark your place in a book

words.example: She used a ribbon as a bookmark.

gloss.en: something that helps or encourages

words.example: The win gave the team a great boost.

gloss.en: a small enclosed space or stall

words.example: We sat in a quiet booth at the back.

gloss.en: the line between two countries

words.example: I live near the Canadian border.

gloss.en: always telling others what to do

words.example: My older sister can be very bossy.

gloss.en: the scientific study of plants

words.example: She studied botany at university.

gloss.en: worried or annoyed about something

words.example: He seemed bothered by the noise.

gloss.en: causing trouble or annoyance

words.example: The flies were really bothersome today.

gloss.en: certain or very likely to happen

words.example: There are bound to be changes soon.

gloss.en: a type of American whisky

words.example: He ordered a glass of bourbon.

gloss.en: a person who fights as a sport

words.example: The boxer trained hard every morning.

gloss.en: a symbol like ( or ) in writing

words.example: Put the extra word in a bracket.

gloss.en: to talk too proudly about yourself

words.example: He likes to brag about his new car.

gloss.en: being brave; courage

words.example: She received a medal for her bravery.

gloss.en: easy to break

words.example: Wrap the breakable items in paper.

gloss.en: the end of a relationship

words.example: She was sad after the breakup.

gloss.en: one of two soft parts on a woman's chest

words.example: She put the baby to her breast.

gloss.en: extremely beautiful or impressive
gloss.zh: 惊人的；令人叹为观止的

words.example: The view from the top was breathtaking.

gloss.en: with a light, pleasant wind

words.example: It was a bright and breezy morning.

gloss.en: a place where beer is made

words.example: We visited a local brewery on holiday.

gloss.en: a hard block used for building

words.example: The school is built of brick.

gloss.en: lasting only a short time

words.example: We had a brief meeting this morning.

gloss.en: for a short time

words.example: He had spoken to Emma only briefly.

gloss.en: in a very clever or bright way

words.example: The sun shone brilliantly all day.

gloss.en: wide

words.example: They walked down a broad street.

gloss.en: a radio or television programme

words.example: We watched the live broadcast of the game.

gloss.en: a person who presents radio or TV shows

words.example: She is a writer and broadcaster.

gloss.en: to make or become wider

words.example: Travel can broaden your mind.

gloss.en: generally, without details

words.example: Broadly speaking, I agree with you.

gloss.en: a small book with pictures and information

words.example: The travel brochure showed sunny beaches.

gloss.en: the brother of your husband or wife

words.example: My brother-in-law works in a bank.

gloss.en: to look through things in a relaxed way

words.example: I like to browse in bookshops.

gloss.en: a blue mark on the skin from a hit

words.example: She had a large bruise on her arm.

gloss.en: a young flower or leaf before it opens

words.example: The roses are still in bud.

gloss.en: a plan of how to spend money

words.example: The company has an annual budget of ten million.

gloss.en: a small insect

words.example: There's a bug crawling up your arm.

gloss.en: an eating disorder with overeating then vomiting

words.example: She suffered from bulimia for years.

gloss.en: a small metal object fired from a gun

words.example: He had a bullet wound in his chest.

gloss.en: to frighten or hurt a weaker person

words.example: Older boys used to bully him at school.

gloss.en: the part you sit on; the bottom

words.example: He fell and landed on his bum.

gloss.en: a bar at the front or back of a car

words.example: The car's front bumper was damaged.

gloss.en: a small round bread or cake

words.example: She ate a warm bun for breakfast.

gloss.en: a group of things joined together

words.example: He bought a bunch of bananas.

gloss.en: causing a lot of work or worry

words.example: The new rules are burdensome for shops.

gloss.en: breaking into a building to steal

words.example: There was a burglary at our neighbour's house.

gloss.en: to break into a place and steal

words.example: Thieves tried to burgle the shop at night.

gloss.en: an injury caused by fire or heat

words.example: She got a burn on her hand from the stove.

gloss.en: a low plant with many stems

words.example: There is a rose bush in the garden.

gloss.en: to move around in a busy way

words.example: Shoppers bustle through the market.

gloss.en: full of busy activity

words.example: The market was bustling with people.

gloss.en: used to show a contrast

words.example: It wasn't the red one but the blue one.

gloss.en: a low continuous humming sound

words.example: I could hear the buzz of bees nearby.

gloss.en: a small room on a ship or plane

words.example: I lay in my cabin feeling seasick.

gloss.en: a wire that carries electricity or signals

words.example: They laid a new fibre-optic cable.

gloss.en: to work out an amount using numbers

words.example: Please calculate the total cost for me.

gloss.en: in a quiet, relaxed way

words.example: She spoke calmly during the emergency.

gloss.en: a planned set of activities for a goal

words.example: They started an anti-smoking campaign.

gloss.en: a man-made waterway for boats

words.example: We took a boat along the Suez Canal.

gloss.en: to decide something will not happen

words.example: All flights have been cancelled because of weather.

gloss.en: a serious disease of growing harmful cells

words.example: He is being treated for lung cancer.

gloss.en: a person applying for a job or election

words.example: She is a strong candidate for the job.

gloss.en: a stick of wax that burns to give light

words.example: She lit a candle in the dark room.

gloss.en: a thin stick used for walking or hitting

words.example: The old man walked with a cane.

gloss.en: a heavy metal ball fired from a cannon

words.example: The old ship fired a heavy cannonball.

gloss.en: a set of accepted important works

words.example: This novel is part of the literary canon.

gloss.en: able to do something

words.example: You are capable of better work than this.

gloss.en: the amount something can hold

words.example: The tank has a capacity of 50 litres.

gloss.en: words printed under a picture

words.example: The photo had a funny caption below it.

gloss.en: a person kept as a prisoner

words.example: The captive was finally set free.

gloss.en: the act of catching a person or thing

words.example: He evaded capture for three days.

gloss.en: a covered vehicle for living or travel

words.example: We spent the summer in a caravan by the sea.
